<argument>
The neighbor’s surprise is best explained by the ordinary surface event: he unexpectedly saw Ernie out watering the lawn. In context, Ernie is secretly putting water into his car’s tank, but when Mr. Jones appears, Ernie instantly covers by pretending he was doing yard work: <quote>Ernie swung the hose around, flipping his thumb over the end to make a spray, and nonchalantly began to water the little rectangle of lawn between sidewalk and curb.</quote>

That matters because Jones is reacting to what he can actually see. The story never says he saw the mysterious powder or understood any “gift.” By contrast, Ernie himself frames the scene afterward as lawn-watering behavior, explaining to his sister: <quote>I've just found out that we're supposed to water our lawns only before seven in the morning or after seven in the evenings. It's the law.</quote>

So the surprise is not that Jones witnessed magic; it is that he encountered Ernie suddenly and implausibly watering the lawn—apparently an unusual sight, especially at dawn and done as a hasty cover story.
</argument>
